2.solution:
On 360-pole track, two runners meet between poles when their relative speed doesn't divide 360.
Option B {2,3,6,13}: opposite pair 3+13=16, 360/16=22.5 (not integer) вҶ’ meets between poles вң“.
Option C {1,4,6,13}: same dir |13вҲ’6|=7, 360/7 not integer вҶ’ meets between poles вң“.
Both B and C satisfy the condition.
Answer: D (More than one).

3.solution:
For no meetings between poles: all pairwise relative speeds must divide 360.
Option B {1,2,3,7}: all differences (1,2,6,1,5,4) divide 360 вң“. All sums (3,4,8,5,9,10) divide 360 вң“.
For any direction assignment, no pair meets between poles.
Answer: B (1,2,3,7).

4.solution:
Trains cross at 2PM (3h after 11AM). Let speeds b (Bangalore) and o (Orissa).
3o/bвҲ’3b/o=4.5 вҶ’ 2kВІ+3kвҲ’2=0 where k=b/o вҶ’ k=1/2 вҶ’ b/o=1/2.
B train arrives: 2PM+3o/b=2PM+6h=8PM. Not among options.
Answer: D (None of these).

6.solution:
Nachiketh left 60min early, arrived 215/11 min early. Time saved=2Г—60Г—w/(v+w)=215/11.
w/(v+w)=43/264. Next day: left 30min early вҶ’ saved=2Г—30Г—43/264=2580/264=215/22=9 17/22 min.
Answer: A (9 17/22).

7.solution:
Late by 20 min, crosses 10 min late. (v_car+v_C)Г—10=20Г—v_C.
вҶ’ v_car=v_C вҶ’ ratio car:Charitha=1:1. Not in options.
Answer: D (None of these).

8.solution:
Angle POQ=270В°CW вҶ’ arc PQ CW=3T/4. First meeting: (v_A+v_B)tвӮҒ=3T/4.
B covers 3Tv_B/(4(v_A+v_B)). Between 1st and 2nd meetings A covers Tv_A/(v_A+v_B).
Condition: 3v_B/4=v_A вҶ’ v_A/v_B=3/4.
A's fraction at 1st meeting: 3TГ—(3/4)/(4Г—7/4)/T=9/28. aГ—b=9Г—28=252.
Answer: 252.

9.solution:
Bike=60, walk=6, D=26km. Bunty drops Anand at d, turns back to pick Chintu.
Chintu walked 2d/11 when picked up. Anand's time: d/60+(26вҲ’d)/6=T.
Bunty's time: (18d/11+26)/60=T вҶ’ solving: d=22km, T=62/60h=62min.
Answer: B (62).

10.solution:
Usual speed=v, D=3v. Late day: 0.9h for first 30% + 2.1v/(vвҲ’16) for rest=3.4h.
2.1v/(vвҲ’16)=2.5 вҶ’ 2.1v=2.5vвҲ’40 вҶ’ v=100km/h. D=300km.
Answer: A (300).
